generic_rules: When inserting contracts, respect AADL scoping rules: component types for component-level properties, implementations for implementation-level properties.
Use visible identifiers (ports, features) in AGREE expressions; avoid references to internal implementation details.
Ensure AGREE expressions use correct data types (int, real, bool) to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` keywords for AGREE contracts.
Employ temporal recurrence for stateful variables (e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`).
Use `if ... then ... else` for branching logic within AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not include AADL properties (e.g., execution time, deadline) directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants.
Avoid using subcomponent paths directly in component-type contracts.
